loading.title: The Reminiscent
loading.description: The project <bold>The Reminiscent</bold> originated years ago from my reading of <link1>John Hejduk’s</link1> <link2>Victims</link2>. That was an conceptual architectural project intended to be realized over thirty years, a monumental tribute to the ordinary souls who perished across turbulent eras. My aspiration is to transpose the scenes described in the book into a series of train carriages. As the train moves slowly forward, it carries those obscure, long-forgotten memories — departing from the old days, traversing the present, and journeying into the depths of eternity.

chapters.head: April 2026, while browsing my bookshelf, I came across a sketchbook I used to carry with me back in 2018. In the top-right corner of those ten or so pages of rough sketches, the title I had written then remained: "John Hejduk - Victims." At that time, relying on the book's descriptions and my own imagination, I had used simple lines to draw the scenes of these ordinary lives. Looking at them, a thought arises: rather than leaving them on paper to gather dust on a shelf or be lost during some future move, why not move them into the carriages of a tram? Let the train carry these forgotten memories, departing from the page and traveling to wherever the network can reach.
chapters.garden: <subtitle>The Horticulturist, the Gardener, the Rosewoman, and the Park Attendant.</subtitle>The Horticulturist remains obsessed with that non-existent piece of black granite. The Gardener, before winter sets in, carefully wraps the shrubs in burlap, listening in the silence to verses of "Botticelli women" and "wings made of pewter." The Rosewoman, with her apricot-coloured eyes, once saw a great snake vanish at the tip of a boot; now, she longs only to wind the stems of black roses around her entire being. As for the Park Attendant, he continues to guard the solitary towers and this secluded garden, amidst the recurring rise and fall of the sun.
